tools.name: search_codebase
tools.description: Search the indexed codebase and return ranked file paths.

tools.name: get_file_context
tools.description: Return file content with dependency context and secret redaction.

tools.name: get_domain_map
tools.description: Return inferred architecture domains from the domain map index.

tools.name: get_index_status
tools.description: Return index freshness and workspace recommendation.

tools.name: index_status
tools.description: Alias for get_index_status. Return index freshness and workspace recommendation.

tools.name: pack_context
tools.description: Query the indexed codebase and pack matching files into a context output. Returns the output file path and token statistics.

token_budget.description: Token budget override (uses config default if omitted)

tools.name: session_clear
tools.description: Delete session deduplication cache files. Pass session_id to clear a specific session, or omit to clear all sessions.

session_id.description: Session ID to clear (omit to clear all)

tools.name: find_callers
tools.description: Find all functions and files that call a given function. Use when user asks 'what calls X', 'who uses this function', 'find callers of Y', 'what invokes Z'.

fn_name.description: The function name to find callers for (e.g. 'authenticate', 'validate_user').

tools.name: impact_analysis
tools.description: Return files that would be affected if a given file is modified. Use when user asks 'what breaks if I change X', 'impact of changing Y', 'who imports Z', 'what depends on file X'.

tools.name: graph_search
tools.description: List all symbols (functions, classes, constants) defined in a given file. Use when user asks 'what is defined in file X', 'list symbols in Y', 'what functions does Z contain'.

tools.name: call_chain
tools.description: Trace the call path between two functions (experimental). Use when user asks 'how does A reach B', 'trace call from X to Y', 'what is the path from function X to function Y'.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Return full readiness status of the ws-ctx-engine server. Shows index state, graph store health, vector backend, node/edge counts, and overall readiness flag. Use this before making queries to verify the server is ready, or when user asks 'is the tool ready?', 'is the index loaded?', 'how many files are indexed?'.

CTX_8b4a40a73e6ab003:content_start:src/ws_ctx_engine/mcp_server.py
from __future__ import annotations

from .mcp import run_mcp_server as _run_mcp_server


def run_mcp_server(
    workspace: str | None = None,
    config_path: str | None = None,
    rate_limit: dict[str, int] | None = None,
) -> None:
    _run_mcp_server(workspace=workspace, config_path=config_path, rate_limit=rate_limit)

CTX_8b4a40a73e6ab003:content_end
